page: Microsoft Azure M192idms_v2 is an isolated, memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the MIDSMediumMemoryv2 family. It is powered by dedicated Intel processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 192 dedicated virtual CPUs. The system is equipped with 4096.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.33 GB per vCPU. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

For local data requirements, the server includes 4194 GB of local disk storage and is premium storage capable. It does not feature any integrated GPUs, making it strictly a CPU-bound and memory-bound resource. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M192idms_v2 represents a high-density hardware class designed to consolidate massive workloads that would otherwise require multiple smaller nodes. The high ratio of memory per core makes it suitable for large-scale memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include large in-memory databases, enterprise-grade relational database management systems, heavy data warehousing, and massive enterprise resource planning systems.
description: Microsoft Azure M192idms_v2 is an isolated, memory-intensive virtual machine from the MIDSMediumMemoryv2 family, designed for massive enterprise workloads. Powered by dedicated Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 dedicated vCPUs and 4096.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 21.33 GB of memory per core. The instance is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 4194 GB of local disk storage with premium storage capabilities. It contains no graphics processing units and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This server is engineered for high-density, memory-bound environments, making it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and enterprise resource planning systems where massive memory capacity and dedicated compute resources are required to maintain performance.
